visa_types.name: Long Stay Visa
visa_types.overview: If you wish to come to the Denmark for more than 90 days, you have to apply for a long stay visa and you apply directly to VFS Global.
visa_types.full_text: If you wish to come to the Denmark for more than 90 days, you have to apply for a long stay visa and you apply directly to VFS Global.

visa_types.name: Studies and Research
visa_types.documents_required:
The Schengen visa allows entry and free movement on the territory of the states covered by the Schengen Agreements. The duration of the short-stay visa not exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days, except for the shorter duration mentioned on the vignette. Visas for stays exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days are not Schengen visas and are subject to special conditions (residence permits, family reunification, etc.)
Click here to download the list of required document
visa_types.visa_fees:
[dnk > sen > en > Table:Business > Visa Fees 1]
Service Fee 29500 XOF
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Please Note:
- The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change.
- The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from 
Additional Services  (additional-services)tab.
The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in XOF.

- Please note that your passport will be kept during the visa application process.
- However, case processing time depends on each individual case. Applicants are advised to apply several weeks before their intended journey, but not more than 90 days in advance.
visa_types.forms:
Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)‘’ .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to submit your documents at VFS. Make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment. Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
A study visa is required for applicants planning to attend a short term educational program in Denmark lasting up to 90 days
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A study visa is required for applicants planning to attend a short term educational program in Denmark lasting up to 90 days

## Visa Fees

[dnk > sen > en > Table:Business > Visa Fees 1]
Service Fee 29500 XOF
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Please Note:
- The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change.
- The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from 
Additional Services  (additional-services)tab.
The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in XOF.

## Documents Required
The Schengen visa allows entry and free movement on the territory of the states covered by the Schengen Agreements. The duration of the short-stay visa not exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days, except for the shorter duration mentioned on the vignette. Visas for stays exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days are not Schengen visas and are subject to special conditions (residence permits, family reunification, etc.)
Click here to download the list of required document

## Photo Specifications
Size of photograph for visa applications
After the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the head should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
The photograph must still be in color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above format.
Applicants may therefore obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
- The legal processing time for a Schengen visa is 15 calendar days.
- Please note that your passport will be kept during the visa application process.
- However, case processing time depends on each individual case. Applicants are advised to apply several weeks before their intended journey, but not more than 90 days in advance.

## Download Forms
Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)‘’ .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to submit your documents at VFS. Make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment. Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you are planning a short visit to Denmark for up to 90 days or less and you do not have the nationality of one of the countries of the European Economic Area (EEA), you may need to apply for a Schengen visa.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you are planning a short visit to Denmark for up to 90 days or less and you do not have the nationality of one of the countries of the European Economic Area (EEA), you may need to apply for a Schengen visa.

## Visa Fees

[dnk > sen > en > Table:Business > Visa Fees 1]
Service Fee 29500 XOF
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Please Note:
- The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change.
- The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from 
Additional Services  (additional-services)tab.
The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in XOF.

## Documents Required
The Schengen visa allows entry and free movement on the territory of the states covered by the Schengen Agreements. The duration of the short-stay visa not exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days, except for the shorter duration mentioned on the vignette. Visas for stays exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days are not Schengen visas and are subject to special conditions (residence permits, family reunification, etc.)
Click here to download the list of required document

## Photo Specifications
Size of photograph for visa applications
After the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the head should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
The photograph must still be in color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above format.
Applicants may therefore obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
- The legal processing time for a Schengen visa is 15 calendar days.
- Please note that your passport will be kept during the visa application process.
- However, case processing time depends on each individual case. Applicants are advised to apply several weeks before their intended journey, but not more than 90 days in advance.

## Download Forms
Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)‘’ .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to submit your documents at VFS. Make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment. Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit
visa_types.documents_required:
You will need to have all the documents listed in the list concerning you, available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark (https://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-residence-permit). Some will have to be provided to you by the person residing in Denmark with whom you are in contact. As an applicant for the residence permit, you will have to obtain the other documents from the file yourself.
Register on the Internet and pay the application fee
Before presenting yourself in person, with your passport and the various documents required, you will have to register on the Internet portal of residence permits. If you apply for the visa for the first time, you will first have to register as a user of this portal.
REGISTER ON THE INTERNET PORTAL OF TITLES OF STAY
Once your application is registered on the Internet portal of residence permits, you will receive confirmation electronically. This confirmation letter will be accompanied by a receipt stating that you have completed the application form and paid the corresponding fees. You will need to print this receipt and attach it to the other documents
visa_types.visa_fees:
Note: From 01 July 2019, The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark including all Danish diplomatic missions are introducing mandatory online payment of the embassy fee for residence or work permit. The embassy fee must be paid online using this link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/payment-for-services/). The receipt for the payment must be submitted at the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) when you hand in your passport, relevant documentation and supply biometrics. The embassy fee and the fee to the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) must be paid in addition to the fee to The Danish Immigration Service.
Please Note:
- The applicable VFS service fee 29500 XOF is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change.
- The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from 
Additional Services (additional-services) tab.
The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in XOF.
visa_types.processing_time:
- Applications for residence permits are subject to review by the Danish Immigration Service or the Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI) . Information on response times is available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark (https://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-residence-permit/). 
answer to your request
If you have been granted a residence permit, you must provide a recent photo, a passport (if you had not submitted it with the application) and a ticket reservation. An entry visa sticker will be placed on your passport to allow you to travel to Denmark.
The VFS is responsible for alerting you by phone once a decision has been made on your application.
If your application has been rejected, you will receive a written response indicating the grounds for refusal. You can lodge an appeal within 3 weeks of receiving the decision.
When you arrive
When you enter the Schengen area, you will need to be able to present:
- Your passport, in which the visa sticker has been affixed (if you are required to hold a visa);
- a copy of your residence permit;
- A relevant document proving the nature of your trip.
As soon as you know your arrival date in Denmark, you will have to make an appointment with the Denmark police. This appointment should take place as soon as possible after your entry on the territory.
The police will photograph you and proceed to order the residence card. This is a small plastic card, with your photo and confirming that you have a residence permit in Denmark. This card will allow you to visit other countries in the Schengen area. Additional information on the applicable regulations in this context can be found on the website of the Directorate of Immigration.
visa_types.forms:
To download or access online application forms, please click here (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Applying).
From 01 July 2019, The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark including all Danish diplomatic missions are introducing mandatory online payment of the embassy fee (https://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/payment-for-services/) for residence or work permit. The embassy fee must be paid online using this link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)
After completing the visa application form (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Applying) and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to submit your documents at VFS. Make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment. Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Need a residence permit to work or settle in Denmark if planned stay more than 90 days (3 months).
The applicable provisions are as follows:
- EU / EEA nationals do not need a residence permit to stay or work in Denmark. On the other hand, all nationals of these countries who stay in Denmark for more than three months must report to the police.
- If you are a national of a non-EU / EEA state and you want to work in Denmark or stay there for more than 90 days, you must have a residence permit.
In general, the the Danish Immigration Service or the Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI) is the body that handles the processing of residence permit applications.
Before applying for a residence permit
You must first determine what type of residence permit corresponds to your needs, depending on the reason for which you are applying, such as work, study, family reunification or an "au pair" position. You will find information on these different residence permits, their criteria for obtaining them and the application procedures on the website of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark (https://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-residence-permit).
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Need a residence permit to work or settle in Denmark if planned stay more than 90 days (3 months).
The applicable provisions are as follows:
- EU / EEA nationals do not need a residence permit to stay or work in Denmark. On the other hand, all nationals of these countries who stay in Denmark for more than three months must report to the police.
- If you are a national of a non-EU / EEA state and you want to work in Denmark or stay there for more than 90 days, you must have a residence permit.
In general, the the Danish Immigration Service or the Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI) is the body that handles the processing of residence permit applications.
Before applying for a residence permit
You must first determine what type of residence permit corresponds to your needs, depending on the reason for which you are applying, such as work, study, family reunification or an "au pair" position. You will find information on these different residence permits, their criteria for obtaining them and the application procedures on the website of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark (https://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-residence-permit).

## Visa Fees
Note: From 01 July 2019, The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark including all Danish diplomatic missions are introducing mandatory online payment of the embassy fee for residence or work permit. The embassy fee must be paid online using this link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/payment-for-services/). The receipt for the payment must be submitted at the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) when you hand in your passport, relevant documentation and supply biometrics. The embassy fee and the fee to the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) must be paid in addition to the fee to The Danish Immigration Service.
Please Note:
- The applicable VFS service fee 29500 XOF is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change.
- The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from 
Additional Services (additional-services) tab.
The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in XOF.

## Documents Required
You will need to have all the documents listed in the list concerning you, available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark (https://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-residence-permit). Some will have to be provided to you by the person residing in Denmark with whom you are in contact. As an applicant for the residence permit, you will have to obtain the other documents from the file yourself.

Before presenting yourself in person, with your passport and the various documents required, you will have to register on the Internet portal of residence permits. If you apply for the visa for the first time, you will first have to register as a user of this portal.
REGISTER ON THE INTERNET PORTAL OF TITLES OF STAY
Once your application is registered on the Internet portal of residence permits, you will receive confirmation electronically. This confirmation letter will be accompanied by a receipt stating that you have completed the application form and paid the corresponding fees. You will need to print this receipt and attach it to the other documents

## Photo Specifications
Size of photograph for visa applications
After the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the head should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
The photograph must still be in color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above format.
Applicants may therefore obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
- Applications for residence permits are subject to review by the Danish Immigration Service or the Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI) . Information on response times is available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark (https://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-residence-permit/). 
answer to your request
If you have been granted a residence permit, you must provide a recent photo, a passport (if you had not submitted it with the application) and a ticket reservation. An entry visa sticker will be placed on your passport to allow you to travel to Denmark.
The VFS is responsible for alerting you by phone once a decision has been made on your application.
If your application has been rejected, you will receive a written response indicating the grounds for refusal. You can lodge an appeal within 3 weeks of receiving the decision.
When you arrive
When you enter the Schengen area, you will need to be able to present:
- Your passport, in which the visa sticker has been affixed (if you are required to hold a visa);
- a copy of your residence permit;
- A relevant document proving the nature of your trip.
As soon as you know your arrival date in Denmark, you will have to make an appointment with the Denmark police. This appointment should take place as soon as possible after your entry on the territory.
The police will photograph you and proceed to order the residence card. This is a small plastic card, with your photo and confirming that you have a residence permit in Denmark. This card will allow you to visit other countries in the Schengen area. Additional information on the applicable regulations in this context can be found on the website of the Directorate of Immigration.

## Download Forms
To download or access online application forms, please click here (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Applying).
From 01 July 2019, The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark including all Danish diplomatic missions are introducing mandatory online payment of the embassy fee (https://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/payment-for-services/) for residence or work permit. The embassy fee must be paid online using this link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)
After completing the visa application form (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Applying) and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to submit your documents at VFS. Make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.

visa_types.overview:

visa_types.name: Cultural and Sport
visa_types.documents_required:
The Schengen visa allows entry and free movement on the territory of the states covered by the Schengen Agreements. The duration of the short-stay visa not exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days, except for the shorter duration mentioned on the vignette. Visas for stays exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days are not Schengen visas and are subject to special conditions (residence permits, family reunification, etc.)
Click here to download the list of required document
visa_types.visa_fees:
[dnk > sen > en > Table:Business > Visa Fees 1]
Service Fee 29500 XOF
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Please Note:
- The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change.
- The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from 
Additional Services  (additional-services)tab.
The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in XOF.
visa_types.processing_time:
- The legal processing time for a Schengen visa is 15 calendar days.
- Please note that your passport will be kept during the visa application process.
- However, case processing time depends on each individual case. Applicants are advised to apply several weeks before their intended journey, but not more than 90 days in advance.
visa_types.forms:
Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)‘’ .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to submit your documents at VFS. Make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment. Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Cultural and Sport visa is required for applicants who intend to participate in cultural or sports events for a short stay of up to 90 days or less.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Cultural and Sport visa is required for applicants who intend to participate in cultural or sports events for a short stay of up to 90 days or less.

## Visa Fees

[dnk > sen > en > Table:Business > Visa Fees 1]
Service Fee 29500 XOF
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Please Note:
- The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change.
- The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from 
Additional Services  (additional-services)tab.
The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in XOF.

## Documents Required
The Schengen visa allows entry and free movement on the territory of the states covered by the Schengen Agreements. The duration of the short-stay visa not exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days, except for the shorter duration mentioned on the vignette. Visas for stays exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days are not Schengen visas and are subject to special conditions (residence permits, family reunification, etc.)
Click here to download the list of required document

## Photo Specifications
Size of photograph for visa applications
After the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the head should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
The photograph must still be in color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above format.
Applicants may therefore obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
- The legal processing time for a Schengen visa is 15 calendar days.
- Please note that your passport will be kept during the visa application process.
- However, case processing time depends on each individual case. Applicants are advised to apply several weeks before their intended journey, but not more than 90 days in advance.

## Download Forms
Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)‘’ .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to submit your documents at VFS. Make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment. Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Business visa is required if during any portion of the visit applicant intends to conduct business or commercial activities. e.g.; Meetings, Conference, Trainings, Trade Fairs, etc; such visas can only be issued to travelers not being paid for work by an entity within Denmark and its representing countries. If the applicant intends to undertake paid work in Denmark and its representing countries, he/she will need to obtain a work permit.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Business visa is required if during any portion of the visit applicant intends to conduct business or commercial activities. e.g.; Meetings, Conference, Trainings, Trade Fairs, etc; such visas can only be issued to travelers not being paid for work by an entity within Denmark and its representing countries. If the applicant intends to undertake paid work in Denmark and its representing countries, he/she will need to obtain a work permit.

## Visa Fees

[dnk > sen > en > Table:Business > Visa Fees 1]
Service Fee 29500 XOF
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Please Note:
- The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change.
- The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from 
Additional Services  (additional-services)tab.
The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in XOF.

## Documents Required
The Schengen visa allows entry and free movement on the territory of the states covered by the Schengen Agreements. The duration of the short-stay visa not exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days, except for the shorter duration mentioned on the vignette. Visas for stays exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days are not Schengen visas and are subject to special conditions (residence permits, family reunification, etc.)
Click here to download the list of required document

## Photo Specifications
Size of photograph for visa applications
After the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the head should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
The photograph must still be in color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above format.
Applicants may therefore obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
- The legal processing time for a Schengen visa is 15 calendar days.
- Please note that your passport will be kept during the visa application process.
- However, case processing time depends on each individual case. Applicants are advised to apply several weeks before their intended journey, but not more than 90 days in advance.

## Download Forms
Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)‘’ .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to submit your documents at VFS. Make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment. Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa for those who intends to visit their cousins, aunt, uncle, distant relatives or friends. If during any portion of their visit to Denmark the applicant intends to engage in business, or official duties he/she must refer to other relevant visa categories.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa for those who intends to visit their cousins, aunt, uncle, distant relatives or friends. If during any portion of their visit to Denmark the applicant intends to engage in business, or official duties he/she must refer to other relevant visa categories.

## Visa Fees

[dnk > sen > en > Table:Business > Visa Fees 1]
Service Fee 29500 XOF
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Please Note:
- The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change.
- The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from 
Additional Services  (additional-services)tab.
The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in XOF.

## Documents Required
The Schengen visa allows entry and free movement on the territory of the states covered by the Schengen Agreements. The duration of the short-stay visa not exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days, except for the shorter duration mentioned on the vignette. Visas for stays exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days are not Schengen visas and are subject to special conditions (residence permits, family reunification, etc.)
Click here to download the list of required document

## Photo Specifications
Size of photograph for visa applications
After the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the head should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
The photograph must still be in color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above format.
Applicants may therefore obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
- The legal processing time for a Schengen visa is 15 calendar days.
- Please note that your passport will be kept during the visa application process.
- However, case processing time depends on each individual case. Applicants are advised to apply several weeks before their intended journey, but not more than 90 days in advance.

## Download Forms
Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)‘’ .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to submit your documents at VFS. Make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment. Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.
